thread.title: 1,000 bikes for police: Groups hail Barau’s commitment to Kano security
thread.title_full: 1,000 bikes for police: Groups hail Barau’s commitment to Kano security

author: Blueprint Newspapers
title: 1,000 bikes for police: Groups hail Barau’s commitment to Kano security
text: The Concerned Kano Forum and the All Progressives Congress (APC) Northwest Youths have applauded the Deputy President of the Senate, Barau Jibrin, for donating 1, 000 motorcycles to the Kano state Police Command. The motorcycles, set to be officially handed over at the Police Barracks in Bompai on November 23, 2024, aim to enhance police operational efficiency and bolster security across the state, particularly in Kano North. The spokesman for the Concerned Kano Forum, Salisu Haruna, in a statement, described the initiative as a “testament” to Senator Barau’s dedication to public welfare and effective leadership. He noted that the motorcycles would improve police mobility, enabling quicker response times and better coverage of remote areas. “This initiative will enhance the police’s capacity to maintain law and order while fostering trust within the community,” Haruna said. Similarly, the APC Northwest Youth Leader, Abdulhamid Umar Muhd, praised the gesture, calling it a “timely intervention” that underscored Barau’s commitment to community safety. He said improved mobility would create a more responsive police force capable of tackling security challenges effectively. “Senator Barau’s initiative demonstrates his dedication to the peace and safety of Kano. It will make a significant difference in reaching underserved areas,” Muhd said.
